medications.indication_rationale: For post-cramp muscle soreness; source advised 1000mg every 6 hours.
medications.dose_route_frequency: 1000mg orally every 6 hours (max 4g/day) (as per source note)

medications.indication_rationale: Replaces sweat fluid/electrolyte losses; source advised electrolyte-rich fluids rather than plain water alone.
Quinine is not indicated for acute exercise-associated cramp (serious haematological risks; reserved, cautiously, for refractory nocturnal cramps under supervision).

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Passively stretch the calf (dorsiflex the foot) and hold; rehydrate with an electrolyte solution; apply gentle heat.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Passive stretch breaks the cramp reflex; electrolyte replacement addresses the contributor.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Avoid vigorous exercise for ~48 hours; resume gradually with adequate hydration and conditioning.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Allows microtrauma to heal and reduces recurrence.

patient_education_points.point: This is an exercise cramp — the muscle fatigued and locked; stretching releases it. Expect a bruised, sore feeling for 2–3 days from the intense contraction.
Rehydrate with an electrolyte drink (not just plain water), rest the leg, and ease back into exercise over a couple of days.

Go to ED if the WHOLE leg becomes swollen, red, hot or tender at rest (possible clot), if the calf becomes tensely swollen with numbness or a cold pale foot, or if your urine turns dark like tea/cola.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Persistent pain suggesting a muscle strain; recurrent cramps; hydration/conditioning.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Whole-leg swelling/warmth, tense calf with numbness/cold foot, or dark urine — ED.

must_include_items: Exclude DVT, compartment syndrome and arterial ischaemia before attributing to cramp
acceptable_alternatives: Gentle heat/massage; magnesium is low-evidence and optional
errors_of_commission: Prescribing quinine for acute exercise cramp
Attributing to 'just a cramp' without a vascular screen
Advising plain-water-only rehydration after heavy sweating as the key measure
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires an explicit DVT/compartment/ischaemia exclusion, conservative management, and a rhabdomyolysis safety-net.
